description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun with a robust wooden stock and foregrip, paired with a matte black metal receiver and barrel assembly. The stock and foregrip are made from dark-stained wood, giving the weapon a vintage appearance, while the rest of the body is constructed from metal with a utilitarian finish. The magazine is a prominent, detachable box type, extending vertically beneath the receiver. The barrel is relatively short and ends with a simple, unadorned muzzle, lacking any advanced muzzle device. Iron sights are mounted on the top, with a rear aperture and a front post for aiming. The overall design is reminiscent of early to mid-20th-century military firearms, built for durability and reliability in urban combat scenarios.
description: A vintage submachine gun with a dark wooden stock and foregrip, matte black metal body, and a detachable box magazine. Features iron sights and a short, unadorned barrel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full wooden stock, foregrip, and the box magazine extending downward.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the barrel's end, iron front sight, and the narrow profile of the magazine.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden stock, foregrip, and the magazine, mirroring the first side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the stock's butt and the alignment of the iron sights along the top.
